moroccoZone.historyText: A complete look back at the memorable 2022 epic and the strategic roadmap established for the 2026 tournament.

item1.title: Showdown in New Jersey: Atlas Lions Finalize Tactics to Face Brazil in Group C Opener
item1.summary: On the eve of their highly anticipated Group C opening match at the MetLife Stadium, the Moroccan National Team held its final closed-door training session.

item2.title: A Historic Start: World Cup 2026 Kicks Off in Mexico City with 48-Team Expansion
item2.summary: The largest-ever FIFA World Cup in history has officially begun at the legendary Estadio Azteca in Mexico City with 48 nations competing.

item3.title: Last-Minute Ticket Rush: FIFA Activates Official Mobile-Only Resale Phase
item3.summary: FIFA has launched its official last-minute and fan-to-fan resale ticket phase on FIFA.com, strictly digital and mobile-only.

item4.title: Rebuilding the Magic: Coach Mohamed Ouahbi Finalizes Morocco's Tactical Blueprint
item4.summary: Morocco's head coach has finalized his strategy, relying on the creative pairing of Brahim Díaz and captain Achraf Hakimi to face Brazil.

item5.title: Canadian Hosts Shine: Fan Festivals Open in Vancouver and Toronto for Group Stage Matches
item5.summary: Canada is ready to host its first-ever men's World Cup matches with upgraded natural turf pitches and vibrant fan zones.

item6.title: MetLife Magic: Global Football Fans Gather in New York/New Jersey Ahead of Group Openers
item6.summary: Times Square and Liberty State Park have turned into colorful hubs of fans from Morocco, Brazil, and Scotland ahead of group stage openers.

item7.title: Tactical Revelation: Achraf Hakimi's Hybrid Role Analyzed by Experts
item7.summary: Analysts dissect Hakimi's positioning, transitioning from right wing-back to central playmaker.

item8.title: Security & Logistics: Free Public Transit for Fans in New York
item8.summary: New York City announces free subway and bus rides for match ticket holders on match days.

item9.title: Azteca on Fire: A Look Back at the Volcanic Opening Match Atmosphere
item9.summary: Over 85,000 spectators ignited the Azteca Stadium to celebrate the return of the global tournament to Mexico.

item10.title: Refereeing Technology: Connected Ball Chip Successfully Tested
item10.summary: The new match ball features a high-precision chip transmitting the exact position 500 times per second.
